hero.title: Security
hero.subtitle: Security is built into every layer of AtonixDev — identity, data, network, and runtime.

architecture.title: Security Architecture
architecture.description: AtonixDev implements security controls across every layer of the platform stack — from identity and access management through to runtime container security and continuous observability.

subsections.title: Identity & Access Control
subsections.body: All user and service access is governed by a centralised identity platform supporting SSO, OAuth 2.0, and RBAC. API keys and tokens are scoped to minimum required permissions and rotated on schedule.
points: SSO via SAML 2.0 and OIDC federation
OAuth 2.0 with scoped token issuance
Role-based access control at every API surface
API key management with expiry and rotation policies

subsections.title: Data Protection
subsections.body: Customer data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS 1.3. Encryption keys are managed through a dedicated key management service with per-tenant key isolation and automatic rotation.
points: AES-256 encryption at rest for all stored data
TLS 1.3 enforced for all data in transit
Per-tenant key isolation in key management service
Automatic key rotation with no service interruption

subsections.title: Application Security
subsections.body: All public-facing applications are protected by a Web Application Firewall with OWASP Top 10 ruleset. Rate limiting, input validation, and output encoding are enforced at every API endpoint.
points: WAF with OWASP Top 10 and custom rules
API rate limiting and throttling per client
Input validation at every API boundary
Secure coding standards enforced via SAST in CI pipelines

subsections.title: Runtime Security
subsections.body: All container images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment and signed with verified digests. Runtime policies prevent privilege escalation, block unexpected system calls, and flag anomalous behaviour.
points: Container image scanning prior to every deployment
Image signing with verified digest enforcement
Runtime policies: no privilege escalation, read-only root FS
Behavioural anomaly detection on running workloads

devPractices.title: Developer Security Practices
devPractices.description: AtonixDev engineers follow a secure development lifecycle from the first line of code through to production operation.

cards.title: Secure Coding Standards
cards.desc: All engineers follow internal secure coding standards aligned to OWASP guidelines. Code review checklists include explicit security gates before any pull request is approved.

cards.title: Dependency Scanning
cards.desc: All third-party dependencies are scanned automatically on every commit. Known CVEs trigger pipeline failures and developer notifications before the change can merge.

cards.title: Static Analysis (SAST)
cards.desc: Static application security testing runs on every pull request. Security findings are categorised by severity and must be resolved before production deployment.

cards.title: Secrets Management
cards.desc: No credentials, API keys, or tokens are stored in source code. All secrets are injected at runtime from a dedicated secrets manager with role-based access and full audit trails.

cards.title: CI/CD Security Gates
cards.desc: Security scans — SAST, dependency scanning, container scanning, and policy checks — are mandatory CI gates. A failed security check blocks promotion through any pipeline stage.

useCases.title: Security Use Cases
useCases.description: Concrete examples of how AtonixDev security capabilities protect workloads and data in production environments.

scenarios.title: Protecting APIs
scenarios.desc: All public APIs are fronted by a WAF enforcing OWASP Top 10 rules, rate limiting per client, and anomaly-based detection. Suspicious patterns trigger automatic blocks with alerts escalated to the security team.

scenarios.title: Securing Multi-Tenant Workloads
scenarios.desc: Each customer's workloads run in isolated namespaces with separate encryption keys, network policies, and RBAC boundaries. Cross-tenant data access is architecturally impossible by design.

scenarios.title: Preventing Unauthorised Access
scenarios.desc: Zero-trust policies require every request — internal or external — to carry a valid, scoped credential. Lateral movement within the platform is blocked by default network and identity policies.

scenarios.title: Detecting Suspicious Activity
scenarios.desc: Security events are collected from all platform layers and processed by a SIEM. Anomaly detection models trigger automated containment and escalation workflows on behavioural deviations.

compliance.title: Compliance & Governance
compliance.description: AtonixDev maintains alignment with internationally recognised security frameworks and implements continuous governance to support enterprise and government audit requirements.

items.title: SOC 2 Principles
points: Security, availability, and confidentiality controls mapped to SOC 2 Trust Service Criteria
Access controls, encryption, and monitoring aligned to SOC 2 requirements
Incident response and change management procedures fully documented
Annual third-party audit readiness maintained

items.title: ISO 27001 Controls
points: Information security policies documented and enforced
Risk assessment and treatment processes in place
Supplier security assessments and access controls maintained
Continuous improvement cycle with documented corrective actions

items.title: Zero-Trust Architecture
points: Verify explicitly — all requests authenticated and authorised regardless of origin
Least privilege — minimal permissions granted per identity and workload
Assume breach — design and operate as if the perimeter is already compromised
Continuous verification — no standing access, session-scoped tokens only

items.title: Audit Logging
points: All privileged actions, access events, and security alerts logged
Logs are tamper-evident and stored in isolated, write-only storage
Audit trails retained for 12 months minimum
Logs available to authorised customers via secure export on request

cta.title: Explore Security Resources
cta.description: Access detailed security documentation, API references, and the AtonixDev Trust Center for compliance and audit information.
